_comment: PROPOSED DEFAULT LIST -- review and edit before relying on this in a real deployment. This is a risk-tolerance judgment call for the operator, not a purely technical one. Autonomy is the default: any action_type NOT listed here runs unattended. Categories follow the four named in the originating spec: irreversible, trust_boundary, ambiguous_authorization, spend_scope_policy.

gates.reason: Permanently destroying data cannot be undone by this system; a human must confirm intent first.
Merging changes history other people build on top of; reverting is possible but disruptive, so treat as effectively irreversible.
Once published, a release is consumed by others; it cannot be quietly un-published.
Anything leaving this system to a real external recipient (email, chat, webhook) cannot be recalled and is also irreversible once sent.
Writing to a repository or account not already explicitly pre-authorized crosses a boundary the operator has not yet agreed to.

Example/demo gated action type used by the fixture workers under examples/ to exercise the full gate -> operator-pending -> resolution path end to end. Remove once real gated action types replace the fixtures, or keep as a template for how to add a new gate.
A worker's own signal that it cannot tell whether it is allowed to do something. Always gated: escalate rather than assume broader permission for itself.
Any action with an associated real-world cost above whatever threshold the operator sets is a spend decision, not an execution decision.

Changes to this file, the fan-out cap, or the pre-approved-repo list are policy decisions about what the system is allowed to do, not task execution -- they must not be able to loosen their own constraints unattended.
